meta.name: AQ-10 Autism Spectrum Quotient
meta.description: The AQ-10 is a brief screening version of the Autism Spectrum Quotient (AQ-50), consisting of 10 items designed to quickly identify individuals who may need further assessment for autism spectrum traits. It evaluates social skills, attention switching, attention to detail, communication, and imagination.
meta.author: Allison, Auyeung & Baron-Cohen (2012)
meta.reference: Allison C, Auyeung B, Baron-Cohen S. Toward brief "Red Flags" for autism screening: The Short Autism Spectrum Quotient and the Short Quantitative Checklist for Autism in toddlers in 1,000 cases and 3,000 controls. J Am Acad Child Adolesc Psychiatry. 2012;51(2):202-212.
meta.instruction: Please read each statement below and select the option that best describes you.

questions.text: I often notice small sounds when others do not
I usually concentrate more on the whole picture, rather than the small details
I find it easy to do more than one thing at once
If there is an interruption, I can switch back to what I was doing very quickly
I find it easy to 'read between the lines' when someone is talking to me
I know how to tell if someone listening to me is getting bored
When I'm reading a story I find it difficult to work out the characters' intentions
I like to collect information about categories of things (e.g. types of car, types of bird, types of train, types of plant, etc.)
I find it easy to work out what someone is thinking or feeling just by looking at their face
I find it difficult to work out people's intentions

scoring.description: For agree items, score 1 if Definitely Agree or Slightly Agree; for disagree items, score 1 if Slightly Disagree or Definitely Disagree

ranges.label: Normal Range
ranges.description: Your score is within the normal range, indicating no significant autism spectrum traits. Your social communication, attention flexibility, and imaginative abilities appear typical.
suggestions: This result suggests you are unlikely to be on the autism spectrum based on this brief screening measure
If you continue to have specific concerns about social interaction or sensory processing, consider discussing them with a healthcare professional
Remember that this is a screening tool and does not replace comprehensive clinical evaluation

ranges.label: Borderline Range
ranges.description: Your score is in the borderline range, suggesting some possible autism spectrum traits in areas such as social communication or attention switching. This does not constitute a diagnosis.
suggestions: This borderline score does not imply a diagnosis but may warrant further self-observation and professional consultation
If these traits cause difficulties in your social relationships, work, or daily routines, consider seeking a comprehensive evaluation
Learning about autism spectrum conditions and neurodiversity can help with greater self-understanding and acceptance
Consider speaking with a psychologist or psychiatrist who specializes in adult autism spectrum assessment

ranges.label: Further Assessment Recommended
ranges.description: Your score is elevated, suggesting notable autism spectrum traits across multiple domains. A comprehensive professional assessment is recommended to determine whether these traits meet diagnostic criteria.
suggestions: Contact a clinical psychologist or psychiatrist experienced in autism spectrum assessment for a comprehensive diagnostic evaluation
This screening result is not a diagnosis and requires in-depth clinical assessment including developmental history and behavioral observation
Understanding your unique cognitive and social profile can help you develop personalized strategies for managing daily challenges
Explore local autism support resources, online communities, and advocacy organizations that can provide information and peer support
Regardless of the diagnostic outcome, identifying your strengths and challenges can improve self-awareness and quality of life
